elements.sent-2411-878: For the last inequality, we have applied Lemma~\ref{lemabc with $a=\beta+\frac{1 {8}$, $b=2\sigma+\frac{11 {24}$ and $c=\sigma$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-879: We can readily verify that \bqs 0<\beta+\frac{1 {8 -\sigma=a-c \, ,\quad 0<\sigma+\frac{11 {24 =b-c \, ,\quad \text{ and \quad b-c+a-1=\beta+\sigma+\frac{1 {8 +\frac{11 {24 -1=\sigma+\beta-\frac{5 {12 \ge 0 \, , \eqs thanks to our assumptions on $\beta+\sigma \ge \frac{5 {12}$ and $0\leq \sigma<\beta+\frac{1 {8}$.}}}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-880: As a consequence (recalling the definition of the constant $C_1$), we have obtained that \bqs \| \mathbf{p ^{n+1 \|_{\ell^1_\beta \le \dfrac{1 {(2+n)^\sigma \, \Big( C_\cL(\beta,\gamma) \, (1+C_m(\gamma)) +\epsilon \, C_1 \Big) \, \left\| \bfh \right\|_{\ell^1_\gamma \le \dfrac{C_0 {(2+n)^\sigma \, \left\| \bfh \right\|_{\ell^1_\gamma \, , \eqs thanks to our choice of $C_0$ and the restrictions on $\epsilon$.}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-881: Using once more that the initial perturbation $\mathbf{p ^{0}\in\ell^1_\gamma$ is of zero mass, we directly find, using the semi-group estimate of $(\cL^n)_{n\in\N}$ in $\ell^\infty_\beta$ that \bqs \left\| \cL^{n+1 \, \mathbf{p ^{0 \right\|_{\ell^\infty_{\beta \, \le \, \dfrac{C_\cL(\beta,\gamma) {(2+n)^{\sigma+\frac{11 {24 \, \|\mathbf{p ^{0}\|_{\ell^1_{\gamma \, \le \, \dfrac{C_\cL(\beta,\gamma) \, (1+C_m(\gamma)) {(2+n)^{\sigma+\frac{11 {24 \, \|\bfh\|_{\ell^1_{\gamma \, , \eqs where we have used one more time the following consequence of \eqref{inegalitesp0 : \begin{equation}\| p^{0} \|_{\ell^1_{\gamma}} \, \le \, (1+C_m(\gamma)) \, \| \bfh \|_{\ell^1_{\gamma}} \, .\end{equation}.}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-882: Regarding the second term, we use Lemma~\ref{lemLtheta with $\nu_1=\beta$ and $\nu_2=\sigma+\frac{25 {24}$ to obtain \begin{equation}\left\| \cL^{n-m} \left(L^\theta -\cL\right) \, p^m\right\|_{\ell^\infty_\beta} \leq \left\| \cL^{n-m} \left(L^\theta -\cL\right) \, p^m\right\|_{\ell^1_\beta} \leq \, \dfrac{C_L\left(\beta,\sigma+\frac{25}{24}\right)}{(n-m+1)^{\sigma+\frac{25}{24}}} \, |\theta| \, \|p^m\|_{\ell^\infty} \leq \, \dfrac{\epsilon \, C_0 \, C_L\left(\beta,\sigma+\frac{25}{24}\right)}{(n-m+1)^{\sigma+\frac{25}{24}} \, (1+m)^{\sigma+\frac{11}{24}}} \, \|\bfh\|_{\ell^1_\gamma} \, .\end{equation}.}}
elements.sent-2411-883: Next, using Lemma~\ref{lemabc with $a=\sigma+\frac{25 {24 >1$ and $b=c=\sigma+\frac{11 {24}$, noticing that $c<a$, we directly obtain that \begin{equation}\left\|\sum_{m=0}^{n} \cL^{n-m} \left( L^\theta -\cL\right) \, p^m \right\|_{\ell^\infty_\beta} \le \, \sum_{m=0}^{n} \dfrac{\epsilon \, C_0 \, C_L\left(\beta,\sigma+\frac{25}{24}\right)}{(n-m+1)^{\sigma+\frac{25}{24}}(1+m)^{\sigma+\frac{11}{24}}} \, \| \bfh \|_{\ell^1_\gamma} \le \, \dfrac{2 \, \epsilon \, C_0 \, C_L\left(\beta,\sigma+\frac{25}{24}\right) \, C \left(\sigma+\frac{25}{24},\sigma+\frac{11}{24},\sigma+\frac{11}{24}\right)}{(2+n)^{\sigma+\frac{11}{24}}} \, \|\bfh\|_{\ell^1_\gamma} \, .\end{equation}.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-884: For the third term, we shall use the estimate~\eqref{estimLnshift2 of Theorem~\ref{thmlinestim and Lemma~\ref{estimNtheta to derive that \begin{equation}\left\| \sum_{m=0}^{\left\lfloor\frac{n+1}{2}\right\rfloor} \cL^{n-m}\left(Id-S\right) \mathscr{N}^\theta(p^m)\right\|_{\ell^\infty_\beta} \leq \sum_{m=0}^{\left\lfloor\frac{n+1}{2}\right\rfloor} \frac{C_\cL(\beta,2\beta)}{(1+n-m)^{\beta+\frac{1}{3}+\min\left(\frac{1}{4},\beta\right)}} \left\|\mathscr{N}^\theta(p^m)\right\|_{\ell^1_{2\beta}} \leq \sum_{m=0}^{\left\lfloor\frac{n+1}{2}\right\rfloor} \frac{C_\cL(\beta,2\beta) C_{\mathscr{N}}(\beta,\varrho)}{(1+n-m)^{\beta+\frac{7}{12}}} \left\|p^m\right\|_{\ell^1_{\beta}}\left\|p^m\right\|_{\ell^\infty_{\beta}} \leq \sum_{m=0}^{\left\lfloor\frac{n+1}{2}\right\rfloor} \frac{C_0^2 \, C_\cL(\beta,2\beta) \, C_{\mathscr{N}}(\beta,\varrho)}{(1+n-m)^{\beta+\frac{7}{12}}(1+m)^{2\sigma+\frac{11}{24}}} \, \left\|\bfh\right\|_{\ell^1_\gamma}^2 \leq \frac{\epsilon \, C_0^2 \, C_\cL(\beta,2\beta) \, C_{\mathscr{N}}(\beta,\varrho) \, C \left(\beta+\frac{7}{12},2\sigma+\frac{11}{24},\sigma+\frac{11}{24}\right)}{(2+n)^{\sigma+\frac{11}{24}}} \, \left\| \bfh \right\|_{\ell^1_\gamma} \, .\end{equation}.}}}
elements.sent-2411-885: For the last inequality, we have used the first inequality of Lemma~\ref{lemabc with $a=\beta+\frac{7 {12}$, $b=2\sigma+\frac{11 {24}$ and $\sigma+\frac{11 {24}$, noticing that \bqs a-c=\beta+\frac{1 {8 -\sigma>0, \quad \text{ and \quad a-c+b-1=\sigma+\beta-\frac{5 {12}\geq 0, \eqs since $0\leq \sigma < \beta+\frac{1 {8}$ and $\beta+\sigma \ge \frac{5 {12}$.}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-886: For the remaining part of the sum, we use estimate~\eqref{estimLnshift3 of Theorem~\ref{thmlinestim and Lemma~\ref{estimNtheta to obtain \begin{equation}\left\| \sum_{m=\left\lfloor\frac{n+1}{2}\right\rfloor+1}^n \cL^{n-m}\left(Id-S\right) \mathscr{N}^\theta(p^m)\right\|_{\ell^\infty_\beta} \leq \sum_{m=\left\lfloor\frac{n+1}{2}\right\rfloor+1}^n \frac{C_\cL(\beta,2\beta)}{(1+n-m)^{\beta+\min\left(\frac{1}{8},\beta-\frac{1}{8}\right)}} \left\|\mathscr{N}^\theta(p^m)\right\|_{\ell^\infty_{2\beta}} \leq \sum_{m=\left\lfloor\frac{n+1}{2}\right\rfloor+1}^n \frac{C_\cL(\beta,2\beta) C_{\mathscr{N}}(\beta,\varrho)}{(1+n-m)^{\beta+\frac{1}{8}}}\left\|p^m\right\|_{\ell^\infty_{\beta}}^2 \leq \sum_{m=\left\lfloor\frac{n+1}{2}\right\rfloor+1}^n \frac{C_\cL(\beta,2\beta) C_{\mathscr{N}}(\beta,\varrho)C_0^2}{(1+n-m)^{\beta+\frac{1}{8}}(1+m)^{2\sigma+\frac{11}{12}}} \left\|\bfh\right\|_{\ell^1_\gamma}^2 \leq \frac{\epsilon \, C_0^2 \, C_\cL(\beta,2\beta) \, C_{\mathscr{N}}(\beta,\varrho) \, C\left(\beta+\frac{1}{8},2\sigma+\frac{11}{12},\sigma+\frac{11}{24}\right)}{(2+n)^{\sigma+\frac{11}{24}}} \, \left\| \bfh \right\|_{\ell^1_\gamma} \, .\end{equation}.}}}
elements.sent-2411-887: For the last inequality, we have used the second inequality of Lemma~\ref{lemabc with $a=\beta+\frac{1 {8}$, $b=2\sigma+\frac{11 {12}$ and $\sigma+\frac{11 {24}$, noticing that \bqs b-c>0, \quad \text{ and \quad a-c+b-1=\sigma+\beta-\frac{5 {12}\geq 0.}}}}}}
elements.chapter-2411-007: The exact and approximate Green's functions for the Cauchy problem
elements.sent-2411-888: In this appendix, we study the Lax-Wendroff scheme when applied to the linear transport equation: \begin{equation}\partial_t v \, + \, a \, \partial_x v \, = \, 0 \, ,\end{equation} with $a \neq 0$ and the equation is considered on the whole real line $\R$.
elements.sent-2411-889: As we have already seen earlier in this article, in the linear case, the Lax-Wendroff scheme can be recast under the form: \begin{equation}\label{transportLW} \forall \, n \in \N \, ,\quad v^{n+1} \, = \, \overline{\mathscr{L}} \, v^n \, ,\end{equation} where for any integer $n \in \N$, $v^n$ denotes the sequence $(v^n_j)_{j \in \Z}$, and $\overline{\mathscr{L }$ is the discrete convolution operator defined on any real or complex valued sequence $v=(v_j)_{j \in \Z}$ by: \begin{equation}\forall \, j \in \Z \, ,\quad (\overline{\mathscr{L}} \, v)_j \, := \, v_j-\dfrac{\alpha}{2} \left( v_{j+1}-v_{j-1} \right) + \dfrac{\alpha^2}{2} \left( v_{j+1}-2v_j+v_{j-1}\right) \, ,\end{equation} where, as in the core of this article, $\alpha$ is a short notation for $\lambda \, a$, $\lambda>0$ denoting the fixed ratio $\Delta t/\Delta x$ between the time and space steps.}
elements.sent-2411-890: The constant $\alpha$ thus has the sign of the transport velocity $a$.
elements.sent-2411-891: In what follows, we shall apply the results below to either $\alpha=\alpha_\ell \in (0,1)$ or $\alpha=\alpha_r \in (-1,0)$, so that the above operator $\overline{\mathscr{L }$ corresponds to either one of the operators $\mathscr{L _\ell$ or $\mathscr{L _r$ defined in \eqref{cauchyLW .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-892: In this appendix, we recall or prove several bounds on the exact and approximate Green's functions for the Lax-Wendroff scheme.
elements.sent-2411-893: The approximate Green's function is defined below in \eqref{A-defGapproxjn and is meant to reproduce the leading qualitative and quantitative features of the exact Green's function of \eqref{transportLW .}}
elements.sent-2411-894: The study of the Green's function of \eqref{transportLW was the purpose of the article \cite{jfcAMBP by one of the authors.}}
elements.sent-2411-895: We shall recall below the main conclusions of \cite{jfcAMBP since they are useful for our purpose here.}
elements.sent-2411-896: Unsurprisingly, many arguments below for studying the approximate Green's function follow what has been already done in \cite{jfcAMBP but there are also several new regimes that need to be considered and that did not appear in \cite{jfcAMBP .}}
elements.sent-2411-897: The bounds that we prove below (see in particular Theorem \ref{thm-A3 ) are used in this article to study the so-called activation function $\mathbf{A}$ in our decomposition of the Green's function for the linearized operator $\mathscr{L}$ in \eqref{linear (see Theorem \ref{thmGreen ) and various bounds for some remainder terms.}}}
elements.sent-2411-898: This appendix can also be seen as a main building block for a complete justification of the local limit theorem for finite difference approximations of the transport equation that exhibit dispersion and dissipation (we refer to \cite{Petrov and \cite{RSF1,RSF2 for a presentation and some recent advances on the local limit theorem and its connection to probability theory).}}
elements.sent-2411-899: The local limit theorem in the non-dispersive (or rather parabolic) case is justified in \cite{CF-CRAS and the complete justification of the local limit theorem in the dispersive case is a work in progress.}
elements.sent-2411-900: We refer to \cite{RSF1 for a justification of the leading order term in the local limit theorem for sequences that exhibit dispersion and dissipation as we consider here.}
elements.section-2411-016: The exact and approximate Green's functions. Main results
elements.sent-2411-901: As we have recalled above, the Lax-Wendroff scheme for the transport equation on the whole real line reads: \begin{equation}\label{A-LWlineaire} v_j^{n+1} \, = \, v_j^n \, - \, \dfrac{\alpha}{2} \, (v_{j+1}^n-v_{j+1}^n) \, + \, \dfrac{\alpha^2}{2} \, (v_{j+1}^n-2\, v_j^n+v_{j+1}^n) \, ,\end{equation} with $\alpha :=\lambda \, a$.
elements.sent-2411-902: The exact Green's function for \eqref{A-LWlineaire corresponds to the initial condition defined by: \begin{equation}\forall \, j \in \Z \, ,\quad \overline{\mathscr{G}}_j^0 \, :=  1 \, , \rm if $j=0$, 0 \, , \rm otherwise, \end{equation} which leads to the solution $(\overline{\mathscr{G _j^n)_{(j,n) \in \Z \times \N}$ for \eqref{A-LWlineaire .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-903: This sequence $(\overline{\mathscr{G _j^n)_{(j,n) \in \Z \times \N}$ is studied in \cite{jfcAMBP , see also \cite{hedstrom1,hedstrom2 .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-904: Its main features are recalled below.
elements.sent-2411-905: We restrict from now on to the case $\alpha \in (-1,1) \setminus \{ 0 \}$ in order to stick to the two particular situations we are interested in, that is, either $\alpha =\alpha_\ell \in (0,1)$ or $\alpha=\alpha_r \in (-1,0)$.
elements.sent-2411-906: We compute the so-called amplification factor for \eqref{A-LWlineaire and obtain (see for instance \cite{jfcAMBP ): \begin{equation}\forall \, \theta \in \R \, ,\quad \widehat{F}_{\rm LW}(\theta) \, = \, 1 \, - \, 2 \, \alpha^2 \, \sin^2 \dfrac{\theta}{2} \, + \, \mbi \, \alpha \, \sin \theta \, .\end{equation}.}}
elements.sent-2411-907: As already reported in \cite{jfcAMBP , the expansion of the amplification factor $\widehat{F _{\rm LW}$ near the frequency $0$ reads: \begin{equation}\label{A-TaylorLW} \widehat{F}_{\rm LW}(\theta) \, = \, \exp \left ( \mathbf{i} \, \alpha \, \theta \, - \, \mathbf{i} \, \dfrac{\alpha \, (1 - \alpha^2)}{6} \, \theta^{\, 3} \, - \, \dfrac{\alpha^2 \, (1 - \alpha^2)}{8} \, \theta^{\, 4} \, + \, \mathcal{O}(\theta^{\, 5}) \right) \, ,\end{equation} and we have furthermore the dissipation property: \begin{equation}\forall \, \theta \in [-\pi,\pi] \setminus \{ 0 \} \, ,\quad |\widehat{F}_{\rm LW}(\theta)| \, < \, 1 \, .\end{equation}.}}
elements.sent-2411-908: Since $\widehat{F _{\rm LW}$ is a trigonometric polynomial, it extends as a holomorphic function with respect to $\theta$ on the whole complex plane $\C$.}
elements.sent-2411-909: For later use, we introduce the coefficients: \begin{equation}\label{A-defc3c4} c_3 \, := \, \dfrac{\alpha \, (1 - \alpha^2)}{6} \neq 0 \, ,\quad c_4 \, := \, \dfrac{\alpha^2 \, (1 - \alpha^2)}{8} \, > \, 0 \, ,\end{equation} in such a way that \eqref{A-TaylorLW equivalently reads: \begin{equation}\widehat{F}_{\rm LW}(\theta) \, = \, \exp \Big( i \, \alpha \, \theta \, - \, i \, c_3 \, \theta^3 \, - \, c_4 \, \theta^4 \, + \, O(\theta^5) \Big) \, ,\end{equation} as $\theta$ tends to $0$.}
elements.formula-2411-272: \begin{equation}\widehat{F}_{\rm LW}(\theta) \, = \, \exp \Big( i \, \alpha \, \theta \, - \, i \, c_3 \, \theta^3 \, - \, c_4 \, \theta^4 \, + \, O(\theta^5) \Big) \, ,\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-910: The main result proved in \cite{jfcAMBP can be formulated as follows.}
elements.sent-2411-911: It uses the notation $c_3$ and $c_4$ for the coefficients in \eqref{A-defc3c4 that arise in the Taylor's expansion of the amplification factor $\widehat{F _{\rm LW}$ at $0$.}}
elements.sent-2411-912: Assume that the constant $c_3$ in \eqref{A-defc3c4 is positive, that is $\alpha \in (0,1)$.}
elements.sent-2411-913: Then there exist two constants $C>0$ and $c>0$ such that the Green's function $(\overline{\mathscr{G _j^n)_{(j,n) \in \Z \times \N}$ for \eqref{A-LWlineaire satisfies the uniform bounds: \begin{equation}\label{thm-A1-bound1} \forall \, n \in \N^* \, ,\quad \forall \, j \in \Z \, ,\quad \big| \, \overline{\mathscr{G}}_j^n \, \big| \, \le \, \dfrac{C}{n^{\, 1/3}} \, \exp \left( - \, c \, \left( \dfrac{j \, - \, \alpha \, n}{n^{\, 1/3}} \right)^{3/2} \, \right) \, ,\quad \text{\rm if } j \, - \, \alpha \, n \, \ge \, 0 \, ,\end{equation} and: \begin{equation}\label{thm-A1-bound2} \forall \, n \in \N^* \, ,\quad \forall \, j \in \Z \, , \big| \, \overline{\mathscr{G}}_j^n \, - \, 2 \, \rm Re  g_j^n \, \big| \, \le \, \dfrac{C}{n^{\, 1/3}} \, \exp \left( - \, c \, \left( \dfrac{|j \, - \, \alpha \, n|}{n^{\, 1/3}} \right)^{3/2} \, \right) \, + \, \dfrac{C}{n^{\, 1/2}} \, \exp \left( - \, c \, \dfrac{(j \, - \, \alpha \, n)^2}{n} \, \right) \, , \rm if  j \, - \, \alpha \, n \, < \, 0 \, ,\end{equation} where $\mathfrak{g _j^n$ is defined for $n \in \N^*$ and $j \in \Z$ as: \begin{equation}\label{thm-A1-profil-principal} \forall \, (n,j) \in \N^* \times \Z \, ,\quad g_j^n \, := \, \dfrac{1}{2 \, \pi} \, \exp \left( - \, \dfrac{c_4 \, (j-\alpha \, n)^2}{9 \, c_3^2 \, n} \, \right) \, \exp \left( i \, \dfrac{2 \, |j-\alpha \, n|^{3/2}}{3 \, \sqrt{3 \, |c_3| \, n}} \, - \, i \, \dfrac{\pi}{4} \right) \times \int_{-\sqrt{\frac{2 \, |j-\alpha \, n|}{3\, |c_3| \, n}}}^{\sqrt{\frac{2 \, |j-\alpha \, n|}{3\, |c_3| \, n}}} \, {\rm e}^{- \, \sqrt{3 \, |c_3| \, |j-\alpha \, n| \, n} \, u^2} \, {\rm e}^{c_3 \, n \, {\rm e}^{-i \pi/4} \, u^3} \, {\rm d}u \, .\end{equation}.}}}}
elements.formula-2411-273: \begin{equation}\label{thm-A1-bound1} \forall \, n \in \N^* \, ,\quad \forall \, j \in \Z \, ,\quad \big| \, \overline{\mathscr{G}}_j^n \, \big| \, \le \, \dfrac{C}{n^{\, 1/3}} \, \exp \left( - \, c \, \left( \dfrac{j \, - \, \alpha \, n}{n^{\, 1/3}} \right)^{3/2} \, \right) \, ,\quad \rm if  j \, - \, \alpha \, n \, \ge \, 0 \, ,\end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-274: \begin{equation}\label{thm-A1-bound2} \forall \, n \in \N^* \, ,\quad \forall \, j \in \Z \, , \big| \, \overline{\mathscr{G}}_j^n \, - \, 2 \, \rm Re  g_j^n \, \big| \, \le \, \dfrac{C}{n^{\, 1/3}} \, \exp \left( - \, c \, \left( \dfrac{|j \, - \, \alpha \, n|}{n^{\, 1/3}} \right)^{3/2} \, \right) \, + \, \dfrac{C}{n^{\, 1/2}} \, \exp \left( - \, c \, \dfrac{(j \, - \, \alpha \, n)^2}{n} \, \right) \, , \rm if  j \, - \, \alpha \, n \, < \, 0 \, ,\end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-275: \begin{equation}\label{thm-A1-profil-principal} \forall \, (n,j) \in \N^* \times \Z \, ,\quad g_j^n \, := \, \dfrac{1}{2 \, \pi} \, \exp \left( - \, \dfrac{c_4 \, (j-\alpha \, n)^2}{9 \, c_3^2 \, n} \, \right) \, \exp \left( i \, \dfrac{2 \, |j-\alpha \, n|^{3/2}}{3 \, \sqrt{3 \, |c_3| \, n}} \, - \, i \, \dfrac{\pi}{4} \right) \times \int_{-\sqrt{\frac{2 \, |j-\alpha \, n|}{3\, |c_3| \, n}}}^{\sqrt{\frac{2 \, |j-\alpha \, n|}{3\, |c_3| \, n}}} \, {\rm e}^{- \, \sqrt{3 \, |c_3| \, |j-\alpha \, n| \, n} \, u^2} \, {\rm e}^{c_3 \, n \, {\rm e}^{-i \pi/4} \, u^3} \, {\rm d}u \, .\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-914: If $c_3$ is negative, the bounds depending on the sign of $j \, - \, \alpha \, n$ should be switched.
elements.sent-2411-915: In order to be absolutely complete, it is important to note that Theorem \ref{thm-A1 above is not exactly the statement that is proved in \cite{jfcAMBP .}}
elements.sent-2411-916: This is because, in the interval between the completion of \cite{jfcAMBP and the present work, an error was found in \cite{jfcAMBP , which made us modify the statement and the proof of the main result in \cite{jfcAMBP .}}}
elements.sent-2411-917: The new statement, that is correct, and whose corollaries follow exactly as in \cite{jfcAMBP , is Theorem \ref{thm-A1 above.}}
elements.sent-2411-918: The error occurred in the proof of the estimate \eqref{thm-A1-bound2 and in the definition \eqref{thm-A1-profil-principal of the approximation $\mathfrak{g _j^n$ that incorporates the damped oscillations of the Green's function.}}}
elements.sent-2411-919: Rather than reproducing the whole proof of Theorem \ref{thm-A1 (only a tiny part of the proof needs to be corrected), we shall rather give the proof of Theorem \ref{thm-A3 below for the approximate Green's function since the analysis of the approximate Green's function needs to incorporate new regimes that were not considered in \cite{jfcAMBP .}}}
elements.sent-2411-920: The proof of the above estimate \eqref{thm-A1-bound2 corresponds to the bound \eqref{A-bound2 below for the approximate Green's function.}}
elements.sent-2411-921: The reader will most certainly experiment no difficulty to adapt the arguments below to derive the statement in \eqref{thm-A1-bound2 , \eqref{thm-A1-profil-principal .}}
elements.sent-2411-922: Let us recall an immediate consequence of Theorem \ref{thm-A1 , see \cite{jfcAMBP for an even more precise statement.}}
elements.sent-2411-923: Let the constant $c_3$ in \eqref{A-defc3c4 be positive, that is, $\alpha \in (0,1)$.}

elements.sent-2411-985: In other words, the Cauchy formula yields: \begin{equation}\label{formule-Cauchy-G} \forall \, \mu \in \R \, ,\quad \forall \, (x,y) \in \R \times \R^{+*} \, ,\quad \mathbf{G}(x,y) \, = \, \dfrac{1}{2 \, \pi} \, \int_\R {\rm e}^{\mbi \, x \, (\mathbf{i}\mu+\theta)} \, {\rm e}^{\mbi \, c_3 \, y \, (\mathbf{i}\mu+\theta)^3} \, {\rm e}^{-c_4 \, y \, (\mathbf{i}\mu+\theta)^4} \, {\rm d}\theta \, .\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-293: \begin{equation}\label{formule-Cauchy-G} \forall \, \mu \in \R \, ,\quad \forall \, (x,y) \in \R \times \R^{+*} \, ,\quad G(x,y) \, = \, \dfrac{1}{2 \, \pi} \, \int_\R {\rm e}^{\mbi \, x \, (i\mu+\theta)} \, {\rm e}^{\mbi \, c_3 \, y \, (i\mu+\theta)^3} \, {\rm e}^{-c_4 \, y \, (i\mu+\theta)^4} \, {\rm d}\theta \, .\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-986: Expanding all quantities within the integral and applying the triangle inequality, we obtain the bound: \begin{equation}\label{borne-Cauchy-G} \forall \, \mu \in \R \, ,\quad \forall \, (x,y) \in \R \times \R^{+*} \, ,\quad |\mathbf{G}(x,y)| \, \le \, \dfrac{{\rm e}^{-x \, \mu +c_3 \, y \, \mu^3 -c_4 \, y \, \mu^4}}{2 \, \pi} \, \int_\R {\rm e}^{-3 \, y \, \mu \, (c_3-2\, c_4 \, \mu) \, \theta^2} \, {\rm e}^{-c_4 \, y \, \theta^4} \, {\rm d}\theta \, .\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-294: \begin{equation}\label{borne-Cauchy-G} \forall \, \mu \in \R \, ,\quad \forall \, (x,y) \in \R \times \R^{+*} \, ,\quad |G(x,y)| \, \le \, \dfrac{{\rm e}^{-x \, \mu +c_3 \, y \, \mu^3 -c_4 \, y \, \mu^4}}{2 \, \pi} \, \int_\R {\rm e}^{-3 \, y \, \mu \, (c_3-2\, c_4 \, \mu) \, \theta^2} \, {\rm e}^{-c_4 \, y \, \theta^4} \, {\rm d}\theta \, .\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-987: This bound is rather crude but it allows to deal with many regimes in $(x,y)$ by optimizing with respect to the free parameter $\mu \in \R$.
elements.sent-2411-988: Let us now consider $x \ge 0$ and let us choose $\mu=\mu_0 :=(x/(3\, c_3 \, y))^{1/2 \ge 0$.}

elements.sent-2411-990: We define once and for all the positive constant $\mathbf{c _\sharp:=3\, c_3^3/(16 \, c_4^2)$.}

elements.sent-2411-1001: Since $f(0)=0$, $f'(0)<0$ and $f$ tends to $+\infty$ at $+\infty$, it appears that $f$ attains its unique global minimum on $\R^+$ at some $\mu>0$ that is characterized by $f'(\mu)=0$.

elements.sent-2411-1050: In this way, we obtain the decomposition: \begin{equation}\label{propA3-decomposition} \mathbf{G}(x,y) \, = \, \varepsilon_1(x,y) \, + \, \varepsilon_2(x,y) \, + \, \mathscr{H}_\flat(x,y) \, + \, \mathscr{H}_\sharp(x,y) \, ,\end{equation} where the four contributions $\varepsilon_1(x,y)$, $\varepsilon_2(x,y)$, $\mathscr{H _\flat(x,y)$ and $\mathscr{H _\sharp(x,y)$ have the following expressions: \begin{equation}\varepsilon_1(x,y) \, = \, \dfrac{{\rm e}^{-x \, \mu+c_3 \, y \, \mu^3-c_4 \, y \, \mu^4}}{2 \, \pi} \, \int_{-\infty}^{-\Xi(\omega)} {\rm e}^{\mbi \, x \, \theta +\mbi \, c_3 \, y \, \theta^3 -\mbi \, 3 \, c_3 \, y \, \mu^2 \, \theta +\mbi \, 4 \, c_4 \, y \, \mu^3 \, \theta -\mbi \, 4 \, c_4 \, y \, \mu \, \theta^3} \, \times {\rm e}^{-3 \, c_3 \, y \, \mu \, \theta^2+6 \, c_4 \, y \, \mu^2 \, \theta^2} \, {\rm e}^{-c_4 \, y \, \theta^4} \, {\rm d}\theta \, ,\end{equation} where the upper bound $\Xi(\omega)$ and the parameter $\mu$ in the expression of $\varepsilon_1(x,y)$ are defined as: \begin{equation}\label{defXiomega} \Xi(\omega) \, := \, 2 \, \sqrt{\dfrac{\omega}{3\, c_3}} \, + \, \dfrac{2\, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega \, ,\quad \mu \, := \, \sqrt{\dfrac{\omega}{3\, c_3}} \, .\end{equation}.}}

elements.sent-2411-1051: The contribution $\varepsilon_2(x,y)$ has the following expression (with the same definition for $\Xi(\omega)$ and $\mu$): \begin{equation}\label{propA3-epsilon2} \varepsilon_2(x,y) \, = \, \dfrac{{\rm e}^{-x \, \mu+c_3 \, y \, \mu^3-c_4 \, y \, \mu^4}}{2 \, \pi} \, \int_{\Xi(\omega)}^{+\infty} {\rm e}^{\mbi \, x \, \theta +\mbi \, c_3 \, y \, \theta^3 -\mbi \, 3 \, c_3 \, y \, \mu^2 \, \theta +\mbi \, 4 \, c_4 \, y \, \mu^3 \, \theta -\mbi \, 4 \, c_4 \, y \, \mu \, \theta^3} \, \times {\rm e}^{-3 \, c_3 \, y \, \mu \, \theta^2+6 \, c_4 \, y \, \mu^2 \, \theta^2} \, {\rm e}^{-c_4 \, y \, \theta^4} \, {\rm d}\theta \, = \, \overline{\varepsilon_1(x,y)} \, .\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-320: \begin{equation}\label{propA3-epsilon2} \varepsilon_2(x,y) \, = \, \dfrac{{\rm e}^{-x \, \mu+c_3 \, y \, \mu^3-c_4 \, y \, \mu^4}}{2 \, \pi} \, \int_{\Xi(\omega)}^{+\infty} {\rm e}^{\mbi \, x \, \theta +\mbi \, c_3 \, y \, \theta^3 -\mbi \, 3 \, c_3 \, y \, \mu^2 \, \theta +\mbi \, 4 \, c_4 \, y \, \mu^3 \, \theta -\mbi \, 4 \, c_4 \, y \, \mu \, \theta^3} \, \times {\rm e}^{-3 \, c_3 \, y \, \mu \, \theta^2+6 \, c_4 \, y \, \mu^2 \, \theta^2} \, {\rm e}^{-c_4 \, y \, \theta^4} \, {\rm d}\theta \, = \, \overline{\varepsilon_1(x,y)} \, .\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-1052: The contribution $\mathscr{H _\flat(x,y)$ is given by: \begin{equation}\label{propA3-Hbemol} \mathscr{H}_\flat(x,y) \, = \, \dfrac{{\rm e}^{-\mbi \, \pi/4}}{2 \, \pi} \, \int_{-\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\frac{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega}^{\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}} {\rm e}^{-\mbi \, \omega \, y \, \Theta(t) +\mbi \, c_3 \, y \, \Theta(t)^3 -c_4 \, y \, \Theta(t)^4} \, {\rm d}t \, ,\end{equation} with: \begin{equation}\label{defThetat} \forall \, t \in \left[ -\sqrt{\dfrac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\dfrac{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega,\sqrt{\dfrac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}} \right] \, , \quad \Theta(t) \, := \, -\sqrt{\dfrac{\omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\dfrac{2 \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega +t \, {\rm e}^{-\mbi \, \pi/4} \, ,\end{equation} which corresponds to the parametrization of the first slanted segment in Figure \ref{fig:contour-oscillations (the one with slope $-\pi/4$).}}
elements.formula-2411-321: \begin{equation}\label{propA3-Hbemol} \mathscr{H}_\flat(x,y) \, = \, \dfrac{{\rm e}^{-\mbi \, \pi/4}}{2 \, \pi} \, \int_{-\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\frac{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega}^{\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}} {\rm e}^{-\mbi \, \omega \, y \, \Theta(t) +\mbi \, c_3 \, y \, \Theta(t)^3 -c_4 \, y \, \Theta(t)^4} \, {\rm d}t \, ,\end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-322: \begin{equation}\label{defThetat} \forall \, t \in \left[ -\sqrt{\dfrac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\dfrac{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega,\sqrt{\dfrac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}} \right] \, , \quad \Theta(t) \, := \, -\sqrt{\dfrac{\omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\dfrac{2 \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega +t \, {\rm e}^{-\mbi \, \pi/4} \, ,\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-1053: The parametrization of the second segment is entirely similar and we obtain the expression: \begin{equation}\mathscr{H}_\sharp(x,y) \, = \, \overline{\mathscr{H}_\flat(x,y)} \, .\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-1054: Let us start with the estimate of $\varepsilon_2(x,y)$ whose expression is given in \eqref{propA3-epsilon2 .}
elements.sent-2411-1055: By using the expression for the parameter $\mu$ and recalling that we have $x=-\omega \, y$, we get: \begin{equation}\varepsilon_2(x,y) \, = \, \dfrac{1}{2 \, \pi} \, \exp \left( \dfrac{4 \, \omega^{3/2} \, y}{3 \, \sqrt{3\, c_3}} -\dfrac{c_4}{9\, c_3^2} \, \omega^2 \, y \right) \, \int_{\Xi(\omega)}^{+\infty} {\rm e}^{\mbi \, \cdots} \times {\rm e}^{-\sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, \theta^2 +\frac{2 \, c_4}{c_3} \, \omega \, y \, \theta^2} \, {\rm e}^{-c_4 \, y \, \theta^4} \, {\rm d}\theta \, ,\end{equation} where the three dots within the integral stand for a real quantity whose precise expression is useless since we are going to use the triangle inequality so that the modulus of this exponential term will be bounded by $1$.
elements.formula-2411-324: \begin{equation}\varepsilon_2(x,y) \, = \, \dfrac{1}{2 \, \pi} \, \exp \left( \dfrac{4 \, \omega^{3/2} \, y}{3 \, \sqrt{3\, c_3}} -\dfrac{c_4}{9\, c_3^2} \, \omega^2 \, y \right) \, \int_{\Xi(\omega)}^{+\infty} {\rm e}^{\mbi \, \cdots} \times {\rm e}^{-\sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, \theta^2 +\frac{2 \, c_4}{c_3} \, \omega \, y \, \theta^2} \, {\rm e}^{-c_4 \, y \, \theta^4} \, {\rm d}\theta \, ,\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-1056: Indeed, applying the triangle inequality yields the bound: \begin{equation}|\varepsilon_2(x,y)| \, \le \, \dfrac{1}{2 \, \pi} \, \exp \left( \dfrac{4 \, \omega^{3/2} \, y}{3 \, \sqrt{3\, c_3}} -\dfrac{c_4}{9\, c_3^2} \, \omega^2 \, y \right) \, \int_{\Xi(\omega)}^{+\infty} {\rm e}^{-\sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, \theta^2 +\frac{2 \, c_4}{c_3} \, \omega \, y \, \theta^2} \, {\rm e}^{-c_4 \, y \, \theta^4} \, {\rm d}\theta \le \, \dfrac{1}{2 \, \pi} \, \exp \left( \dfrac{4 \, \omega^{3/2} \, y}{3 \, \sqrt{3\, c_3}} \right) \, \int_{\Xi(\omega)}^{+\infty} {\rm e}^{-\sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, \theta^2 +\frac{2 \, c_4}{c_3} \, \omega \, y \, \theta^2} \, {\rm e}^{-c_4 \, y \, \theta^4} \, {\rm d}\theta \, .\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-325: \begin{equation}|\varepsilon_2(x,y)| \, \le \, \dfrac{1}{2 \, \pi} \, \exp \left( \dfrac{4 \, \omega^{3/2} \, y}{3 \, \sqrt{3\, c_3}} -\dfrac{c_4}{9\, c_3^2} \, \omega^2 \, y \right) \, \int_{\Xi(\omega)}^{+\infty} {\rm e}^{-\sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, \theta^2 +\frac{2 \, c_4}{c_3} \, \omega \, y \, \theta^2} \, {\rm e}^{-c_4 \, y \, \theta^4} \, {\rm d}\theta \le \, \dfrac{1}{2 \, \pi} \, \exp \left( \dfrac{4 \, \omega^{3/2} \, y}{3 \, \sqrt{3\, c_3}} \right) \, \int_{\Xi(\omega)}^{+\infty} {\rm e}^{-\sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, \theta^2 +\frac{2 \, c_4}{c_3} \, \omega \, y \, \theta^2} \, {\rm e}^{-c_4 \, y \, \theta^4} \, {\rm d}\theta \, .\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-1057: We first restrict $\omega=|x|/y$ by imposing: \begin{equation}\label{propA3-restriction1} \omega \, \le \, \dfrac{3\, c_3^3}{16 \, c_4^2} \, ,\end{equation} so that we have: \begin{equation}\dfrac{2 \, c_4}{c_3} \, \omega \, \le \, \dfrac{1}{2} \, \sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, .\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-1058: We thus get: \begin{equation}|\varepsilon_2(x,y)| \, \le \, \dfrac{1}{2 \, \pi} \, \exp \left( \dfrac{4 \, \omega^{3/2} \, y}{3 \, \sqrt{3\, c_3}} \right) \, \int_{\Xi(\omega)}^{+\infty} {\rm e}^{-\frac{\sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega}}{2} \, y \, \theta^2} \, {\rm e}^{-c_4 \, y \, \theta^4} \, {\rm d}\theta \le \, \dfrac{1}{2 \, \pi} \, \exp \left( \dfrac{4 \, \omega^{3/2} \, y}{3 \, \sqrt{3\, c_3}} \right) \, \int_{\Xi(\omega)}^{+\infty} {\rm e}^{-\frac{\sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega}}{2} \, y \, \theta^2} \, {\rm d}\theta \le \, \dfrac{1}{2 \, \pi} \, \exp \left( \dfrac{4 \, \omega^{3/2} \, y}{3 \, \sqrt{3\, c_3}} \right) \, \int_{2 \, \sqrt{\frac{\omega}{3\, c_3}}}^{+\infty} {\rm e}^{-\frac{\sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega}}{2} \, y \, \theta^2} \, {\rm d}\theta \, ,\end{equation} where the final inequality comes from the definition of $\Xi(\omega)$.
elements.formula-2411-328: \begin{equation}|\varepsilon_2(x,y)| \, \le \, \dfrac{1}{2 \, \pi} \, \exp \left( \dfrac{4 \, \omega^{3/2} \, y}{3 \, \sqrt{3\, c_3}} \right) \, \int_{\Xi(\omega)}^{+\infty} {\rm e}^{-\frac{\sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega}}{2} \, y \, \theta^2} \, {\rm e}^{-c_4 \, y \, \theta^4} \, {\rm d}\theta \le \, \dfrac{1}{2 \, \pi} \, \exp \left( \dfrac{4 \, \omega^{3/2} \, y}{3 \, \sqrt{3\, c_3}} \right) \, \int_{\Xi(\omega)}^{+\infty} {\rm e}^{-\frac{\sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega}}{2} \, y \, \theta^2} \, {\rm d}\theta \le \, \dfrac{1}{2 \, \pi} \, \exp \left( \dfrac{4 \, \omega^{3/2} \, y}{3 \, \sqrt{3\, c_3}} \right) \, \int_{2 \, \sqrt{\frac{\omega}{3\, c_3}}}^{+\infty} {\rm e}^{-\frac{\sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega}}{2} \, y \, \theta^2} \, {\rm d}\theta \, ,\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-1059: We then use the inequality: \begin{equation}\forall \, a>0 \, ,\quad \forall \, X>0 \, ,\quad \int_X^{+\infty} {\rm e}^{-a \, \theta^2} \, {\rm d}\theta \, \le \, \dfrac{1}{2\, a \, X} \, {\rm e}^{-a \, X^2} \, ,\end{equation} to obtain (for a suitable constant $C$): \begin{equation}|\varepsilon_2(x,y)| \, \le \, \dfrac{C}{\omega \, y} \, \exp \left( \dfrac{4 \, \omega^{3/2} \, y}{3 \, \sqrt{3\, c_3}} \right) \, \exp \left( -\dfrac{2 \, \omega^{3/2} \, y}{\sqrt{3\, c_3}} \right) \, = \, \dfrac{C}{\omega \, y} \, \exp \left( -\dfrac{2 \, \omega^{3/2} \, y}{3 \, \sqrt{3\, c_3}} \right) \, ,\end{equation} and this gives, going back to $x$ and $y$, the estimate: \begin{equation}|\varepsilon_2(x,y)| \, \le \, \dfrac{C}{|x|} \, \exp \left( -c \, \dfrac{|x|^{3/2}}{y^{1/2}} \right) \, .\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-329: \begin{equation}\forall \, a>0 \, ,\quad \forall \, X>0 \, ,\quad \int_X^{+\infty} {\rm e}^{-a \, \theta^2} \, {\rm d}\theta \, \le \, \dfrac{1}{2\, a \, X} \, {\rm e}^{-a \, X^2} \, ,\end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-330: \begin{equation}|\varepsilon_2(x,y)| \, \le \, \dfrac{C}{\omega \, y} \, \exp \left( \dfrac{4 \, \omega^{3/2} \, y}{3 \, \sqrt{3\, c_3}} \right) \, \exp \left( -\dfrac{2 \, \omega^{3/2} \, y}{\sqrt{3\, c_3}} \right) \, = \, \dfrac{C}{\omega \, y} \, \exp \left( -\dfrac{2 \, \omega^{3/2} \, y}{3 \, \sqrt{3\, c_3}} \right) \, ,\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-1060: Restricting to the regime $x\le -y^{1/3}$, we obtain the final estimate: \begin{equation}\label{propA3-estimation1} |\varepsilon_2(x,y)| \, \le \, \dfrac{C}{y^{1/3}} \, \exp \left( -c \, \dfrac{|x|^{3/2}}{y^{1/2}} \right) \, ,\end{equation} as long as we have $x \le -y^{1/3}$ and $|x|/y \le 3\, c_3^3/(16 \, c_4^2)$.
elements.sent-2411-1061: Of course a similar estimate holds for $\varepsilon_1(x,y)$ since it is the complex conjugate of $\varepsilon_2(x,y)$.
elements.sent-2411-1062: We now turn to the contribution $\mathscr{H _\flat(x,y)$ in \eqref{propA3-Hbemol .}}
elements.sent-2411-1063: With the definition \eqref{defThetat for $\Theta(t)$, we expand: \begin{equation}-\mbi \, \omega \, y \, \Theta(t) +\mbi \, c_3 \, y \, \Theta(t)^3 -c_4 \, y \, \Theta(t)^4 \, = \, y \, \sum_{k=0}^4 p_k(\omega) \, t^k \, ,\end{equation} where the quantities $p_0(\omega),\dots,p_4(\omega)$ have the following behavior\footnote{Here we see that our choice of contour only uses approximate saddle points because $p_1(\omega)$ is not zero.}}
elements.formula-2411-333: \begin{equation}-\mbi \, \omega \, y \, \Theta(t) +\mbi \, c_3 \, y \, \Theta(t)^3 -c_4 \, y \, \Theta(t)^4 \, = \, y \, \sum_{k=0}^4 p_k(\omega) \, t^k \, ,\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-1064: This does not matter so much since $p_1(\omega)$ will be small enough to produce error terms that can be controlled in our analysis.
elements.sent-2411-1065: We now use the inequality: \begin{equation}\label{inegexp} \forall \, z \in \C \, ,\quad |{\rm e}^z -1| \, \le \, |z| \, {\rm e}^{|z|} \, ,\end{equation} with the specific value: \begin{equation}z \, := \, y \, \Big\{ p_1(\omega) \, t \, + \, (p_2(\omega) +\sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega}) \, t^2 \, + \, (p_3(\omega) -c_3 \, {\rm e}^{\, - \, i \, \pi / 4}) \, t^3 \, + \, p_4(\omega) \, t^4 \Big\} \, .\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-335: \begin{equation}z \, := \, y \, \Big\{ p_1(\omega) \, t \, + \, (p_2(\omega) +\sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega}) \, t^2 \, + \, (p_3(\omega) -c_3 \, {\rm e}^{\, - \, i \, \pi / 4}) \, t^3 \, + \, p_4(\omega) \, t^4 \Big\} \, .\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-1066: On the considered integration interval for $t$, we thus have (see \eqref{p1 , \eqref{partiereellep2 , \eqref{partieimagp2 , \eqref{p3 , \eqref{p4 ): \begin{equation}|z| \, \le \, C \, y \, \big( \omega^{5/2} \, + \, \omega \, t^2 \big) \, ,\end{equation} with a constant $C$ that is independent of $y$, $\omega$ and $t$ (for the relevant values of $t$).}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-1067: This gives the estimate: \begin{equation}\left| \mathscr{H}_\flat(x,y) \, - \, \dfrac{{\rm e}^{p_0(\omega) \, y-\mbi \, \pi/4}}{2 \, \pi} \, \int_{-\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\frac{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega}^{\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}} \exp \Big( - \sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, t^2 +\mbi \, c_3 \, {\rm e}^{\, - \, i \, \pi / 4} \, y \, t^3 \Big) \, {\rm d}t \right| \le C \, y \, {\rm e}^{\rm Re  p_0(\omega) \, y +C \, \omega^{5/2} \, y} \, \int_{-\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\frac{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega}^{\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}} \big( \omega^{5/2} \, + \, \omega \, t^2 \big) \, {\rm e}^{- \sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, t^2 +\frac{c_3}{\sqrt{2}} \, y \, t^3 +C \, \omega \, y \, t^2} \, {\rm d}t \, .\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-337: \begin{equation}\left| \mathscr{H}_\flat(x,y) \, - \, \dfrac{{\rm e}^{p_0(\omega) \, y-\mbi \, \pi/4}}{2 \, \pi} \, \int_{-\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\frac{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega}^{\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}} \exp \Big( - \sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, t^2 +\mbi \, c_3 \, {\rm e}^{\, - \, i \, \pi / 4} \, y \, t^3 \Big) \, {\rm d}t \right| \le C \, y \, {\rm e}^{\rm Re  p_0(\omega) \, y +C \, \omega^{5/2} \, y} \, \int_{-\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\frac{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega}^{\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}} \big( \omega^{5/2} \, + \, \omega \, t^2 \big) \, {\rm e}^{- \sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, t^2 +\frac{c_3}{\sqrt{2}} \, y \, t^3 +C \, \omega \, y \, t^2} \, {\rm d}t \, .\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-1068: We first use \eqref{partiereellep0 to estimate the real part of $p_0(\omega)$ and choose $\omega$ small enough so that we have: \begin{equation}\rm Re  p_0(\omega) +C \, \omega^{5/2} \, \le \, - c \, \omega^2 \, ,\end{equation} for a suitable constant $c>0$ that does not depend on $\omega$.}
elements.sent-2411-1069: We thus get the estimate: \begin{equation}\left| \mathscr{H}_\flat(x,y) \, - \, \dfrac{{\rm e}^{p_0(\omega) \, y-\mbi \, \pi/4}}{2 \, \pi} \, \int_{-\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\frac{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega}^{\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}} \exp \Big( - \sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, t^2 +\mbi \, c_3 \, {\rm e}^{\, - \, i \, \pi / 4} \, y \, t^3 \Big) \, {\rm d}t \right| \le C \, y \, {\rm e}^{-c \, \omega^2 \, y} \, \int_{-\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\frac{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega}^{\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}} \big( \omega^{5/2} \, + \, \omega \, t^2 \big) \, {\rm e}^{-\sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, t^2 +\frac{c_3}{\sqrt{2}} \, y \, t^3 +C \, \omega \, y \, t^2} \, {\rm d}t \, .\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-339: \begin{equation}\left| \mathscr{H}_\flat(x,y) \, - \, \dfrac{{\rm e}^{p_0(\omega) \, y-\mbi \, \pi/4}}{2 \, \pi} \, \int_{-\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\frac{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega}^{\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}} \exp \Big( - \sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, t^2 +\mbi \, c_3 \, {\rm e}^{\, - \, i \, \pi / 4} \, y \, t^3 \Big) \, {\rm d}t \right| \le C \, y \, {\rm e}^{-c \, \omega^2 \, y} \, \int_{-\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\frac{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega}^{\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}} \big( \omega^{5/2} \, + \, \omega \, t^2 \big) \, {\rm e}^{-\sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, t^2 +\frac{c_3}{\sqrt{2}} \, y \, t^3 +C \, \omega \, y \, t^2} \, {\rm d}t \, .\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-1070: As in \cite{jfcAMBP , let us now observe that when $t$ is nonpositive in the integral, we have $c_3 \, t^3 \le 0$ (recall that $c_3$ is positive), and when $t$ is positive, on the considered integration interval, we have: \begin{equation}\dfrac{c_3}{\sqrt{2}} \, t^3 \, \le \, \sqrt{\dfrac{c_3 \, \omega}{3}} \, t^2 \, .\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-1071: If $\omega$ is chosen sufficiently small (and this choice is, of course, independent of $y$), we thus get: \begin{equation}{\rm e}^{-\sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, t^2 +\frac{c_3}{\sqrt{2}} \, y \, t^3 +C \, \omega \, y \, t^2} \, \le \, {\rm e}^{-c \, \sqrt{\omega} \, y \, t^2} \, ,\end{equation} for the relevant values of $t$.
elements.formula-2411-341: \begin{equation}{\rm e}^{-\sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, t^2 +\frac{c_3}{\sqrt{2}} \, y \, t^3 +C \, \omega \, y \, t^2} \, \le \, {\rm e}^{-c \, \sqrt{\omega} \, y \, t^2} \, ,\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-1072: This gives the estimate: \begin{equation}\left| \mathscr{H}_\flat(x,y) \, - \, \dfrac{{\rm e}^{p_0(\omega) \, y-\mbi \, \pi/4}}{2 \, \pi} \, \int_{-\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\frac{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega}^{\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}} \exp \Big( - \sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, t^2 +\mbi \, c_3 \, {\rm e}^{\, - \, i \, \pi / 4} \, y \, t^3 \Big) \, {\rm d}t \right| \le C \, y \, {\rm e}^{-c \, \omega^2 \, y} \, \int_{-\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\frac{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega}^{\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}} \big( \omega^{5/2} \, + \, \omega \, t^2 \big) \, {\rm e}^{-c \, \sqrt{\omega} \, y \, t^2} \, {\rm d}t \, .\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-342: \begin{equation}\left| \mathscr{H}_\flat(x,y) \, - \, \dfrac{{\rm e}^{p_0(\omega) \, y-\mbi \, \pi/4}}{2 \, \pi} \, \int_{-\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\frac{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega}^{\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}} \exp \Big( - \sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, t^2 +\mbi \, c_3 \, {\rm e}^{\, - \, i \, \pi / 4} \, y \, t^3 \Big) \, {\rm d}t \right| \le C \, y \, {\rm e}^{-c \, \omega^2 \, y} \, \int_{-\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\frac{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega}^{\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}} \big( \omega^{5/2} \, + \, \omega \, t^2 \big) \, {\rm e}^{-c \, \sqrt{\omega} \, y \, t^2} \, {\rm d}t \, .\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-1073: Integrating now with respect to $t$ (over $\R$ rather than over the above compact interval), we thus have: \begin{equation}\left| \mathscr{H}_\flat(x,y) \, - \, \dfrac{{\rm e}^{p_0(\omega) \, y-\mbi \, \pi/4}}{2 \, \pi} \, \int_{-\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\frac{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega}^{\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}} \exp \Big( - \sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, t^2 +\mbi \, c_3 \, {\rm e}^{\, - \, i \, \pi / 4} \, y \, t^3 \Big) \, {\rm d}t \right| \le C \, y^{1/2} \, \omega^{9/4} \, {\rm e}^{-c \, \omega^2 \, y} \, + \, \dfrac{C}{\sqrt{y}} \, \omega^{1/4} \, {\rm e}^{-c \, \omega^2 \, y} \, \le \, \dfrac{C}{\sqrt{y}} \, (1+\omega^2 \, y) \, {\rm e}^{-c \, \omega^2 \, y} \, .\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-343: \begin{equation}\left| \mathscr{H}_\flat(x,y) \, - \, \dfrac{{\rm e}^{p_0(\omega) \, y-\mbi \, \pi/4}}{2 \, \pi} \, \int_{-\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\frac{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega}^{\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}} \exp \Big( - \sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, t^2 +\mbi \, c_3 \, {\rm e}^{\, - \, i \, \pi / 4} \, y \, t^3 \Big) \, {\rm d}t \right| \le C \, y^{1/2} \, \omega^{9/4} \, {\rm e}^{-c \, \omega^2 \, y} \, + \, \dfrac{C}{\sqrt{y}} \, \omega^{1/4} \, {\rm e}^{-c \, \omega^2 \, y} \, \le \, \dfrac{C}{\sqrt{y}} \, (1+\omega^2 \, y) \, {\rm e}^{-c \, \omega^2 \, y} \, .\end{equation}

elements.sent-2411-1081: This gives the estimate: \begin{equation}\left| \dfrac{{\rm e}^{-\frac{c_4 \, x^2}{9 \, c_3^2 \, y}} \, {\rm e}^{\mbi \, \frac{2 \, |x|^{3/2}}{3 \, \sqrt{3 \, c_3} \, y^{1/2}} -\mbi \, \pi/4}}{2 \, \pi} \, \int_{-\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}-\frac{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} \, \omega}^{-\sqrt{\frac{2 \, \omega}{3 \, c_3}}} \exp \Big( - \sqrt{3 \, c_3 \, \omega} \, y \, t^2 +\mbi \, c_3 \, {\rm e}^{\, - \, i \, \pi / 4} \, y \, t^3 \Big) \, {\rm d}t \right| \le \, C \, \omega \, {\rm e}^{-c \, \omega^2 \, y} \, {\rm e}^{-c \, \omega^{3/2} \, y} \, \le \, \dfrac{C}{\omega^{1/2} \, y} \, (\omega^{3/2} \, y) \, {\rm e}^{-c \, \omega^{3/2} \, y} \, .\end{equation}.

elements.sent-2411-1097: The integral is estimated by cutting at $\theta_0>0$ such that $3 \, c_3 \, |\mu_0|=(c_4/4) \, \theta_0^2$, so that we have: \begin{equation}\int_{\theta_0}^{+\infty} {\rm e}^{3 \, c_3 \, y \, |\mu_0| \, \theta^2} \, {\rm e}^{-\frac{c_4}{2} \, y \, \theta^4} \, {\rm d}\theta \, = \, \int_{\theta_0}^{+\infty} {\rm e}^{\frac{c_4}{4} \, y \, \theta_0^2 \, \theta^2} \, {\rm e}^{-\frac{c_4}{2} \, y \, \theta^4} \, {\rm d}\theta \, \le \, \int_{\theta_0}^{+\infty} {\rm e}^{-\frac{c_4}{4} \, y \, \theta^4} \, {\rm d}\theta \, \le \, \dfrac{C}{y^{1/4}} \, ,\end{equation} and we also have: \begin{equation}\int_0^{\theta_0} {\rm e}^{3 \, c_3 \, y \, |\mu_0| \, \theta^2} \, {\rm e}^{-\frac{c_4}{2} \, y \, \theta^4} \, {\rm d}\theta \, \le \, \int_0^{\theta_0} {\rm e}^{3 \, c_3 \, y \, |\mu_0| \, \theta^2} \, {\rm d}\theta \, \le \, \theta_0 \, {\rm e}^{3 \, c_3 \, y \, |\mu_0| \, \theta_0^2} \, \le \, C \, |\mu_0|^{1/2} \, {\rm e}^{C \, y \, \mu_0^2} \, .\end{equation}.

elements.sent-2411-1160: We have already seen the relation: \begin{equation}\int_{-\infty}^x G(\xi,y) \, {\rm d}\xi \, = \, 1-\int_x^{\underline{c} \, y} G(\xi,y) \, {\rm d}\xi \, - \, \int_{\underline{c} \, y}^{+\infty} G(\xi,y) \, {\rm d}\xi \, ,\end{equation} and we thus already have from the arguments above (namely, the case $x \ge \mathbf{c \, y$): \begin{equation}\left| \int_{-\infty}^x G(\xi,y) \, {\rm d}\xi \right| \, \le \, C \, + \, \int_x^{\underline{c} \, y} |G(\xi,y)| \, {\rm d}\xi \, .\end{equation}.}
elements.formula-2411-395: \begin{equation}\int_{-\infty}^x G(\xi,y) \, {\rm d}\xi \, = \, 1-\int_x^{\underline{c} \, y} G(\xi,y) \, {\rm d}\xi \, - \, \int_{\underline{c} \, y}^{+\infty} G(\xi,y) \, {\rm d}\xi \, ,\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-1161: The final integral on the right-hand side is estimated by using the second case in \eqref{A-bound1 , which gives the uniform control (after an obvious change of variable in $\xi$): \begin{equation}\left| \int_{-\infty}^x G(\xi,y) \, {\rm d}\xi \right| \, \le \, C \, .\end{equation}.}

elements.sent-2411-1163: It thus remains to examine the case $x \in [-\mathbf{c \, y,-y^{1/3 ]$ for which we need to take into account the oscillating behavior of the Green's function.}}

elements.sent-2411-1165: Let therefore $x \in [-\mathbf{c \, y,-y^{1/3 ]$.. We decompose: \begin{equation}\int_{-\infty}^x G(\xi,y) \, {\rm d}\xi \, = \, \int_{-\infty}^{-\underline{c} \, y} G(\xi,y) \, {\rm d}\xi \, + \, \int_{-\underline{c} \, y}^x \big( G(\xi,y)-2 \, \rm Re  g(\xi,y) \big) \, {\rm d}\xi \, + \, 2 \, \rm Re  \int_{-\underline{c} \, y}^x g(\xi,y) \, {\rm d}\xi \, .\end{equation}.}}
elements.formula-2411-399: \begin{equation}\int_{-\infty}^x G(\xi,y) \, {\rm d}\xi \, = \, \int_{-\infty}^{-\underline{c} \, y} G(\xi,y) \, {\rm d}\xi \, + \, \int_{-\underline{c} \, y}^x \big( G(\xi,y)-2 \, \rm Re  g(\xi,y) \big) \, {\rm d}\xi \, + \, 2 \, \rm Re  \int_{-\underline{c} \, y}^x g(\xi,y) \, {\rm d}\xi \, .\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-1166: The first term on the right-hand side has already been estimated, see \eqref{estimprimitive1 , and the second term on the right-hand side is estimated by using the bound\footnote{The whole point of \eqref{A-bound2 was precisely to extract from $\mathbf{G}$ its leading oscillating behavior so that the difference between $\mathbf{G}$ and this ``leading order term'' would actually become uniformly integrable.. \eqref{A-bound2 .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-1167: At this stage, we already have an estimate that reads: \begin{equation}\label{estimprimitive2} \left| \int_{-\infty}^x \mathbf{G}(\xi,y) \, {\rm d}\xi \right| \, \le \, C \, + \, 2 \, \left| \int_{-\underline{\mathbf{c}} \, y}^x \mathfrak{g}(\xi,y) \, {\rm d}\xi \right| \, ,\end{equation} and the remaining point of the proof is to derive a uniform estimate for the primitive function of the explicit function $\mathfrak{g}$ whose expression is given in \eqref{deffrakg .}
elements.formula-2411-400: \begin{equation}\label{estimprimitive2} \left| \int_{-\infty}^x G(\xi,y) \, {\rm d}\xi \right| \, \le \, C \, + \, 2 \, \left| \int_{-\underline{c} \, y}^x g(\xi,y) \, {\rm d}\xi \right| \, ,\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-1168: The proof relies on integration by parts, as detailed below.
elements.sent-2411-1169: We first introduce the notation: \begin{equation}\beta_0 \, := \, \dfrac{c_4}{9 \, c_3^2} >0 \, ,\quad \beta_1 \, := \, \dfrac{2}{3 \, \sqrt{3 \, c_3}} >0 \, ,\end{equation} so that, after performing a change of variable in the integral of \eqref{deffrakg , we obtain the expression: \begin{equation}\label{expressiong1} \mathfrak{g}(x,y) \, := \, \dfrac{3 \, \beta_1 \, {\rm e}^{-\mathbf{i} \, \pi/4}}{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, \pi} \, \dfrac{|x|^{1/2}}{y^{1/2}} \exp \left( - \, \beta_0 \, \dfrac{x^2}{y} \, \right) \, \exp \left( \mathbf{i} \, \beta_1 \, \dfrac{|x|^{3/2}}{y^{1/2}} \right) \int_{-1}^1 \, {\rm e}^{- \, 3 \, \beta_1 \, \frac{|x|^{3/2}}{y^{1/2}} \, u^2} \, {\rm e}^{\beta_1 \, (1-\mbi) \, \frac{|x|^{3/2}}{y^{1/2}} \, u^3} \, {\rm d}u \, .\end{equation}.}
elements.formula-2411-402: \begin{equation}\label{expressiong1} g(x,y) \, := \, \dfrac{3 \, \beta_1 \, {\rm e}^{-i \, \pi/4}}{2 \, \sqrt{2} \, \pi} \, \dfrac{|x|^{1/2}}{y^{1/2}} \exp \left( - \, \beta_0 \, \dfrac{x^2}{y} \, \right) \, \exp \left( i \, \beta_1 \, \dfrac{|x|^{3/2}}{y^{1/2}} \right) \int_{-1}^1 \, {\rm e}^{- \, 3 \, \beta_1 \, \frac{|x|^{3/2}}{y^{1/2}} \, u^2} \, {\rm e}^{\beta_1 \, (1-\mbi) \, \frac{|x|^{3/2}}{y^{1/2}} \, u^3} \, {\rm d}u \, .\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-1170: Let us define a function $H$ on $\R^+$ as follows (the constant $\beta_1>0$ being fixed as above): \begin{equation}\label{deffunctionH} \forall \, w \ge 0 \, ,\quad H(w) \, := \exp \left( \mathbf{i} \, \beta_1 \, w \right) \, \int_{-1}^1 {\rm e}^{- \, 3 \, \beta_1 \, w \, u^2} \, {\rm e}^{\beta_1 \, (1-\mathbf{i}) \, w \, u^3} \, {\rm d}u \, .\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-403: \begin{equation}\label{deffunctionH} \forall \, w \ge 0 \, ,\quad H(w) \, := \exp \left( i \, \beta_1 \, w \right) \, \int_{-1}^1 {\rm e}^{- \, 3 \, \beta_1 \, w \, u^2} \, {\rm e}^{\beta_1 \, (1-i) \, w \, u^3} \, {\rm d}u \, .\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-1171: With the help of \eqref{deffunctionH , we can rewrite \eqref{expressiong1 and obtain the relation: \begin{equation}\label{expressiong2} \forall \, (x,y) \in \R \times \R^{+*} \, ,\quad y^{1/2} \, \mathfrak{g}(y^{1/2} \, x,y) \, := \, \dfrac{\beta_1 \, {\rm e}^{-\mathbf{i} \, \pi/4}}{\sqrt{2} \, \pi} \, {\rm e}^{-\beta_0 \, x^2} \, \left( \dfrac{3}{2} \, y^{1/4} \, |x|^{1/2} \right) \, H(y^{1/4} \, |x|^{3/2}) \, .\end{equation}.}}
elements.formula-2411-404: \begin{equation}\label{expressiong2} \forall \, (x,y) \in \R \times \R^{+*} \, ,\quad y^{1/2} \, g(y^{1/2} \, x,y) \, := \, \dfrac{\beta_1 \, {\rm e}^{-i \, \pi/4}}{\sqrt{2} \, \pi} \, {\rm e}^{-\beta_0 \, x^2} \, \left( \dfrac{3}{2} \, y^{1/4} \, |x|^{1/2} \right) \, H(y^{1/4} \, |x|^{3/2}) \, .\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-1172: We are thus in a very favorable position for applying integration by parts.
elements.sent-2411-1173: Before going further, we prove the following Lemma.
elements.sent-2411-1174: Let the function $H$ be defined on $\R^+$ by \eqref{deffunctionH .}
elements.sent-2411-1175: Then there exists a constant $C>0$ that only depends on $\beta_1$ and such that: \begin{equation}\forall \, w \ge 0 \, ,\quad \left| \int_0^w \, H(w') \, {\rm d}w' \right| \, \le \, C \, .\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-1177: By applying the Fubini Theorem, we get the relation: \begin{equation}\int_0^w \, H(w') \, {\rm d}w' \, = \, \int_{-1}^1 \int_0^w \, \exp \Big( \beta_1 \, w' \, \big( -3 \, u^2 +u^3 \, + \, i \, (1-u^3) \big) \Big) \, {\rm d}w' \, {\rm d}u \, ,\end{equation} and it turns out that the continuous function: \begin{equation}u \in [-1,1] \longmapsto -3 \, u^2 +u^3 \, + \, i \, (1-u^3) \, ,\end{equation} does not vanish.
elements.formula-2411-406: \begin{equation}\int_0^w \, H(w') \, {\rm d}w' \, = \, \int_{-1}^1 \int_0^w \, \exp \Big( \beta_1 \, w' \, \big( -3 \, u^2 +u^3 \, + \, i \, (1-u^3) \big) \Big) \, {\rm d}w' \, {\rm d}u \, ,\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-1178: Its modulus is thus uniformly bounded from below.
elements.sent-2411-1179: We get: \begin{equation}\int_0^w \, H(w') \, {\rm d}w' \, = \, \int_{-1}^1 \dfrac{\exp \Big( \beta_1 \, w \, \big( -3 \, u^2 +u^3 \, + \, i \, (1-u^3) \big) \Big)-1}{\beta_1 \, \big( -3 \, u^2 +u^3 \, + \, i \, (1-u^3) \big)} \, {\rm d}u \, ,\end{equation} and the triangle inequality (as well as a lower bound for the modulus of the denominator) yields: \begin{equation}\left| \int_0^w \, H(w') \, {\rm d}w' \right| \, \le \, C \, \int_{-1}^1 \exp \big( \beta_1 \, w \, (-3 \, u^2 +u^3) \big)+1 \, {\rm d}u \, .\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-408: \begin{equation}\int_0^w \, H(w') \, {\rm d}w' \, = \, \int_{-1}^1 \dfrac{\exp \Big( \beta_1 \, w \, \big( -3 \, u^2 +u^3 \, + \, i \, (1-u^3) \big) \Big)-1}{\beta_1 \, \big( -3 \, u^2 +u^3 \, + \, i \, (1-u^3) \big)} \, {\rm d}u \, ,\end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-409: \begin{equation}\left| \int_0^w \, H(w') \, {\rm d}w' \right| \, \le \, C \, \int_{-1}^1 \exp \big( \beta_1 \, w \, (-3 \, u^2 +u^3) \big)+1 \, {\rm d}u \, .\end{equation}

elements.sent-2411-1181: We now go back to our main problem, which is to prove a bound for the primitive function of $\mathfrak{g}$, see \eqref{estimprimitive2 .}
elements.sent-2411-1182: We use the expression \eqref{expressiong2 and obtain: \begin{equation}\int_{-\underline{c} \, y}^x g(\xi,y) \, {\rm d}\xi \, = \, \int_{-\underline{c} \, y^{1/2}}^{x/y^{1/2}} y^{1/2} \, g(y^{1/2} \, w,y) \, {\rm d}w \, = \, \dfrac{\beta_1 \, {\rm e}^{-i \, \pi/4}}{\sqrt{2} \, \pi} \, \int_{|x|/y^{1/2}}^{\underline{c} \, y^{1/2}} {\rm e}^{-\beta_0 \, w^2} \, \left( \dfrac{3}{2} \, y^{1/4} \, w^{1/2} \, H(y^{1/4} \, w^{3/2}) \right) \, {\rm d}w \, = \, \dfrac{\beta_1 \, {\rm e}^{-i \, \pi/4}}{\sqrt{2} \, \pi} \, \int_{|x|/y^{1/2}}^{\underline{c} \, y^{1/2}} {\rm e}^{-\beta_0 \, w^2} \, \dfrac{{\rm d}}{{\rm d}w} \left( \int_0^{y^{1/4} \, w^{3/2}} \, H(w') \, {\rm d}w' \right) \, {\rm d}w \, .\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-1183: It then only remains to integrate by parts the final integral and to apply Lemma \ref{lemA-1 in order to derive the uniform bound: \begin{equation}\left| \int_{-\underline{c} \, y}^x g(\xi,y) \, {\rm d}\xi \right| \, \le \, C \, .\end{equation}.}

elements.sent-2411-1188: The analysis of Section \ref{section4-3 uses the following quantities defined for any $j_0 \in \N^*$ and $n \in \N^*$: \begin{equation}A_r^n(j_0) \ := \, \dfrac{1}{2 \, \mbi \, \pi} \, \int_{\eta+\mbi \, \R} {\rm e}^{n \, \tau -j_0 \, \varphi_r(\tau)} \, \dfrac{{\rm d}\tau}{\tau} \, ,\end{equation} where $\eta$ is any positive number (the Cauchy formula shows that the definition is independent of $\eta$) and: \begin{equation}B_r^n(j_0) \ := \, \dfrac{1}{2 \, \mbi \, \pi} \, \int_{\mbi \, \R} {\rm e}^{n \, \tau -j_0 \, \varphi_r(\tau)} \, {\rm d}\tau \, .\end{equation}.}

elements.sent-2411-1205: Then for any constant $\mathbf{c >0$, there exist two positive constants $C$ and $c$ such that for any $n \in \N^*$ and any $j_0 \in \N^*$, there holds: \begin{equation}\left| \mathbf{A}_r (-j_0+n\, |\alpha_r|,n) \right| \, \le \begin{cases} C \, \exp \left( -c \, \dfrac{|j_0-n\, |\alpha_r||^{4/3}}{n^{1/3}} \right) \, , \text{\rm if $-j_0+n\, |\alpha_r| \le -\underline{\mathbf{c}} \, n$,} C \, , \text{\rm if $-\underline{\mathbf{c}} \, n \le -j_0+n\, |\alpha_r| \le \underline{\mathbf{c}} \, n$.} \end{cases}\end{equation} and: \begin{equation}\left| 1 - \mathbf{A}_r (-j_0+n\, |\alpha_r|,n) \right| \, \le \, C \, \exp \left( -c \, \dfrac{|j_0-n\, |\alpha_r||^{4/3}}{n^{1/3}} \right) \, ,\quad \text{\rm if $ -j_0+n\, |\alpha_r| \ge \underline{\mathbf{c}} \, n$.}\end{equation} In particular, there holds : \begin{equation}\sup_{j_0 \in \Z \, , \, n \in \N^*} \quad \left| \mathbf{A}_r (-j_0+n\, |\alpha_r|,n) \right| \, < \, + \infty \, .\end{equation}.}
